sanskrit: यज्ञदानतपःकर्म न त्याज्यं कार्यमेव तत्। यज्ञो दानं तपश्चैव पावनानि मनीषिणाम्॥
transliteration: yajña-dāna-tapaḥ-karma na tyājyaṃ kāryam eva tat | yajño dānaṃ tapaś caiva pāvanāni manīṣiṇām ||
literal_meaning: Sacrifice, charity, penance-action not to be abandoned; indeed it is to be done. Sacrifice, charity and penance certainly purify the wise.
contextual_meaning: Even after deep self-inquiry, the psyche still needs structured, prosocial action to stay clean; abandoning ritual, generosity and disciplined effort collapses ego-integration.
psychological_state: Post-insight stabilization: the mind knows the Self is beyond action, yet retains healthy ego-functions through service, giving and self-discipline.
trigger_scenario: After a silent retreat you feel ‘above’ daily chores and donations; inbox overflows, friends feel abandoned, yet you rationalize ‘I’m detached’. Verse reminds: keep the ritual, write the cheque, run the mile—purification is relational.
anchor_text: Keep doing good, giving, and growing—ritual, generosity, and discipline keep the mind clear.
